title: Rich Malay Turkey Curry
description: A cultured, velvety curry featuring turkey mince and confit-tomato, perfect for diabetic-friendly meal prep.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Add diced onion and cook until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in minced garlic and grated ginger, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Fold in curry paste and sauté for another 2 minutes to bloom the spices.
Add turkey mince to the pan and cook, breaking it up, until no longer pink, about 5 minutes.
Pour in confit tomato and coconut milk, stirring to combine.
Season with lime juice, fish sauce, and salt, then bring to a gentle simmer.
Reduce heat to low and let the curry cook uncovered for 10 minutes, allowing flavors to meld.
Stir in chopped cilantro and discard if desired for garnish.
Serve hot over steamed rice or cauliflower rice for a low-carb option.
